explanations.2: This creates a SOAP envelope, which is a standard XML wrapper used to send messages to web services; think of it like an envelope that holds your message.
explanations.4: The 'urn:' prefix and the xmlns attribute define a namespace, which is a way to avoid naming conflicts when XML elements from different sources are combined together.
explanations.5: The %COUNTRY% and %VATNUMBER% are placeholders that will be replaced with actual values before this message is sent to the VAT checking service.
